skill.name: isms-audit-expert
skill.description: Provides ISO 27001 ISMS audit guidance for audit planning, control assessment, compliance verification, and certification preparation.
skill.summary: Plan and document ISO 27001 ISMS audits with structured security control guidance.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by security-audit terminology, markdown code fences, placeholder asset text, and generated marketplace prose. I found no evidence of credential access, Windows SAM database access, network reconnaissance, weak cryptographic implementation, or command execution. The package contains one placeholder Python script, so the remaining risk is low and publishable.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Flags
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to documentation text, file extension examples, or generated marketplace prose. No evidence found of a cryptographic algorithm implementation or insecure cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are prose about audits, examples, or marketplace text. There is no code path that selects or executes cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive Windows SAM Database Flags
low_findings.description: The sensitive-data findings point to audit terminology and generated analysis text. No evidence found of Windows SAM file paths, registry hive access, credential dumping, or credential extraction logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SKILL.md line is a heading for auditor competency, and marketplace_content.json contains generated evaluation prose. Neither file contains credential store access.

low_findings.title: False Positive Shell Backtick Execution Flags
low_findings.description: The external command findings in SKILL.md are markdown code fences and inline filenames used for documentation. No evidence found of shell execution, Ruby backtick execution, subprocess calls, or user-controlled command construction in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged SKILL.md locations are documentation diagrams or resource listings. They do not contain executable shell syntax beyond markdown formatting.

low_findings.title: False Positive Reconnaissance Flags
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings point to audit program language and anti-pattern guidance. No evidence found of network scanning commands, host discovery, port scanning, or external network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines discuss audit KPIs and content guidance. They contain no scanner invocation, target enumeration, or network I/O.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Script Present
low_findings.description: The package contains an executable Python helper placeholder. The script only prints a message and has no network, filesystem, environment, or command execution behavior, but scripts increase review surface.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script body is visible and only calls print from main. The low risk is from the presence of an executable helper, not from harmful behavior.

content.value_statement: ISO 27001 audits require structured evidence, clear scope, and consistent control assessment. This skill helps teams plan ISMS audits, assess controls, and prepare certification evidence.
actual_capabilities: Build risk-based ISMS audit plans for ISO 27001 scopes.
Map audit activities to organizational, technical, physical, and operational controls.
Prepare evidence requests for Stage 1 and Stage 2 certification audits.
Draft security findings with risk impact and remediation guidance.
Review cloud security audit topics against shared responsibility expectations.
Define audit performance metrics for continuous ISMS improvement.
limitations: Does not perform live vulnerability scanning or penetration testing.
Does not replace an accredited ISO 27001 certification auditor.
Requires organization-specific scope, assets, policies, and evidence.
Includes placeholder supporting files rather than complete audit templates.

use_cases.title: Plan Internal ISMS Audits
use_cases.description: Create a risk-based audit schedule, scope, and evidence plan for an internal ISO 27001 program.

use_cases.title: Prepare Certification Evidence
use_cases.description: Organize documentation and control evidence before an external ISO 27001 Stage 1 or Stage 2 audit.

use_cases.title: Assess Control Effectiveness
use_cases.description: Review whether security controls are designed, implemented, and operating as expected.

prompt_templates.title: Start an Audit Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic ISO 27001 ISMS audit plan for a small organization. Include scope, objectives, evidence types, and schedule.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Control Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ISMS control assessment checklist for access control, cryptography governance, logging, backup, and incident response.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Certification
prompt_templates.prompt: Review our ISO 27001 certification readiness. Identify Stage 1 documentation needs, Stage 2 evidence needs, likely gaps, and remediation priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Risk-Based Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a twelve-month ISMS audit program using asset criticality, incident history, control maturity, and regulatory obligations as planning inputs.

output_examples.input: Plan an annual ISMS audit for a mid-sized SaaS company.
output: Audit scope covering cloud operations, access management, incident response, vendor risk, and secure development.
Quarterly reviews for high-risk systems and annual reviews for stable administrative processes.
Evidence requests for policies, risk treatment plans, access reviews, backup tests, incident records, and management reviews.

output_examples.input: Help prioritize ISMS audit findings after a control review.
output: Critical findings affect core confidentiality, integrity, or availability controls and need immediate ownership.
Major findings show missing or ineffective processes and need dated remediation plans.
Minor findings indicate documentation gaps or inconsistent evidence and can follow the normal improvement cycle.

output_examples.input: Prepare our team for an ISO 27001 Stage 1 audit.
output: Confirm the ISMS scope, risk assessment method, Statement of Applicability, and policy approval records.
Check that mandatory documents align with implemented processes and management review outputs.
Prepare a gap list with owners, target dates, and evidence needed before Stage 2.

best_practices: Define audit criteria and scope before requesting evidence.
Keep audit independence separate from remediation ownership.
Link each finding to specific evidence and a clear control expectation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat a generic checklist as proof of control effectiveness.
Do not ignore physical, administrative, and governance controls.
Do not use this skill as a substitute for accredited certification judgment.

faq.question: What does this skill help audit?
faq.answer: It helps audit an information security management system against ISO 27001 planning, evidence, control, and improvement expectations.

faq.question: Can it certify an organization?
faq.answer: No. Certification requires an accredited certification body. This skill supports preparation and internal review.

faq.question: Does it run security scans?
faq.answer: No. It provides audit guidance and planning. It does not perform live scanning, exploitation, or system access.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Internal auditors, compliance managers, security governance teams, and consultants preparing ISMS audit work can use it.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Useful inputs include ISMS scope, asset list, risk register, Statement of Applicability, policies, prior findings, and audit objectives.

faq.question: Can it support cloud audits?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help structure cloud security audit questions around access, logging, encryption governance, configuration, and shared responsibility.
